name: Heaven
description_short: London's most iconic gay megaclub under the Charing Cross arches, running since 1979.
description_long: Heaven has anchored London's gay scene for over four decades. Set beneath the arches of Charing Cross, the three-floor venue hosts pop-heavy club nights, live acts, and regular drag performances. Best known for its weekly G-A-Y late night.

name: Royal Vauxhall Tavern
description_short: Historic London LGBTQ+ institution featuring drag, cabaret, and performance.
description_long: The Royal Vauxhall Tavern is a legendary venue for drag and cabaret performances, beloved by locals and tourists alike.

name: Eagle London
description_short: Leather and bear bar in Vauxhall with late-night dancing.
description_long: Eagle London is a leather bar catering to bears and masculine-identified patrons, with a strong emphasis on community and nightlife.

name: Fire
description_short: Afterhours techno club in Vauxhall.
description_long: Fire is known for its underground techno events and late-night dancing scene.

name: Comptons of Soho
description_short: Historic Soho neighborhood bar beloved by locals and tourists.
description_long: Comptons is an iconic Soho fixture with a warm, welcoming atmosphere.

name: The Admiral Duncan
description_short: Casual historic Soho bar with mixed LGBTQ+ clientele.
description_long: The Admiral Duncan is a staple of Soho nightlife.

name: G-A-Y Bar
description_short: Popular Soho pop bar for tourists and party-goers.
description_long: G-A-Y Bar is a mainstream pop venue in the heart of Soho.

name: Ku Bar Soho
description_short: Upscale cocktail bar in Soho with mixed clientele.
description_long: Ku Bar offers a more refined atmosphere than typical Soho venues.

name: The Glory
description_short: Underground drag and cabaret venue in Haggerston.
description_long: The Glory is known for avant-garde performances and drag shows.

name: Dalston Superstore
description_short: Mixed queer venue with indie vibe and dancefloor.
description_long: Dalston Superstore blends bar, club, and arts space.

name: Industry Bar
description_short: Popular mainstream gay bar in Hell's Kitchen.
description_long: Industry Bar is a staple of NYC nightlife with energetic dancefloor.

name: The Eagle NYC
description_short: Rooftop leather bar with bear scene.
description_long: The Eagle NYC features a popular rooftop and strong leather community.

name: Boxers HK
description_short: Sports bar in Hell's Kitchen with LGBTQ+ crowd.
description_long: Boxers is a casual sports bar popular with tourists and locals alike.

name: Pieces Bar
description_short: Drag bar in Village with drag performances.
description_long: Pieces Bar is famous for drag shows and high-energy atmosphere.

name: Stonewall Inn
description_short: Historic LGBTQ+ landmark and birthplace of the modern movement.
description_long: Stonewall Inn is the iconic site of the 1969 uprising.

name: Hardware Bar NYC
description_short: Bear and leather bar in Hell's Kitchen.
description_long: Hardware Bar is a casual neighborhood spot for bear community.

name: House of Yes
description_short: Immersive performance space in Bushwick.
description_long: House of Yes is known for avant-garde performances and theatrical experiences.

name: Metropolitan Bar
description_short: Casual neighborhood bar in Williamsburg.
description_long: Metropolitan Bar is beloved by Brooklyn locals.

name: Gym Bar NYC
description_short: Sports bar in Chelsea.
description_long: Gym Bar is a casual sports bar in the heart of Chelsea.

name: Nowadays
description_short: Underground techno club in Ridgewood.
description_long: Nowadays is known for cutting-edge electronic music and queer community.

name: Berghain
description_short: Legendary Berlin techno club with strict door policy.
description_long: Berghain is an iconic underground techno venue in an ex-power station.

name: SchwuZ
description_short: Queer nightclub with themed parties and drag shows.
description_long: SchwuZ is a Berlin institution for queer club culture.

name: Ficken 3000
description_short: Cruising bar in Kreuzberg.
description_long: Ficken 3000 is a local favorite for bear and leather community.

name: Möbel Olfe
description_short: Underground queer bar in Kreuzberg.
description_long: Möbel Olfe is beloved by Berlin's queer underground scene.

name: Roses
description_short: Quirky queer bar with kitsch aesthetic.
description_long: Roses is a beloved neighborhood bar with unique charm.

name: KitKatClub
description_short: Fetish and electronic music club.
description_long: KitKatClub is known for provocative parties and boundary-pushing events.

name: Prinzknecht
description_short: Leather bear bar in Schöneberg.
description_long: Prinzknecht is a long-standing bear bar in Berlin.

name: Tresor
description_short: Iconic underground techno club in Mitte.
description_long: Tresor is a legendary Berlin institution for electronic music.

name: CSA Bar
description_short: Casual neighborhood queer bar.
description_long: CSA Bar is a low-key local favorite in Schöneberg.

name: Connection Club
description_short: Circuit club in Schöneberg.
description_long: Connection is a destination for circuit party enthusiasts.

name: Why Not?
description_short: Popular pop club in Chueca.
description_long: Why Not? is a mainstream destination in Madrid's gay district.

name: Black & White
description_short: Mainstream bar in Chueca with mixed clientele.
description_long: Black & White is a popular spot for tourists in Chueca.

name: Leather Club Madrid
description_short: Leather bar in Chueca.
description_long: Leather Club caters to Madrid's bear and leather community.

name: Cool Bar
address: Calle de Isabel la Católica 6, 28013 Madrid
description_short: Upscale cocktail bar.
description_long: Cool Bar offers craft cocktails and sophisticated atmosphere.

name: Fabrik
address: Autovía A-42, Km 12,5, 28970 Humanes de Madrid
description_short: Massive circuit club outside Madrid.
description_long: Fabrik is a legendary destination for circuit parties.

name: LGTB Pride Bar
description_short: Casual bar in Chueca.
description_long: Pride Bar is a welcoming neighborhood spot.

name: Ohm Club
description_short: Circuit club near Plaza de Callao.
description_long: Ohm Club is a popular destination for party-goers.

name: Escape Club Madrid
description_short: Pop club in Chueca.
description_long: Escape Club is a local favorite for dancing.

name: Contact Bar Madrid
description_short: Bear bar in Chueca.
description_long: Contact Bar is a laid-back spot for the bear community.

name: WE Party Madrid
description_short: International circuit party promoter.
description_long: WE Party produces massive events across Madrid venues.

name: Club NYX
description_short: Popular club in Amsterdam's gay district.
description_long: Club NYX is a mainstream venue on Reguliersdwarsstraat.

name: Cafe Reality
description_short: Casual neighborhood bar.
description_long: Cafe Reality is a beloved local spot on the gay street.

name: Prik
description_short: Cocktail bar with queer-friendly vibe.
description_long: Prik is a neighborhood favorite for craft cocktails.

name: Club Church
description_short: Leather-oriented club.
description_long: Club Church caters to Amsterdam's leather and bear community.

name: Eagle Amsterdam
description_short: Leather bar in Red Light District.
description_long: Eagle Amsterdam is a late-night leather bar.

name: Soho Amsterdam
description_short: Mainstream bar in gay district.
description_long: Soho Amsterdam is a popular tourist spot.

name: Queen's Head
description_short: Drag bar with performances.
description_long: Queen's Head is known for drag shows and performances.

name: The Web
description_short: Leather bar in Red Light District.
description_long: The Web is a long-standing leather bar.

name: Spijkerbar
description_short: Bear bar on Kerkstraat.
description_long: Spijkerbar is a local bear bar in Amsterdam.

name: Cockring
description_short: Leather cruise club.
description_long: Cockring is a venue for leather and cruise culture.

name: DJ Station
description_short: Bangkok's best-known multi-floor gay club on Silom Soi 2, famous for nightly drag shows.
description_long: DJ Station is the anchor of Bangkok's Silom gay strip, drawing a mixed tourist and local crowd nightly. Three floors of pop music and drag performances make it a rite of passage for visitors. Peak crowd from midnight to 2am.

name: Balcony Bar
description_short: Open-fronted bar on Silom Soi 4, perfect for street watching and a relaxed pre-club drink.
description_long: One of the most relaxed spots on the Silom strip, Balcony Bar offers an open terrace and casual vibe that draws early-evening crowds before they head to the clubs. Popular with tourists and expats alike.

name: G Bangkok
description_short: High-energy dance club on Silom with a shirtless crowd and EDM-heavy programming.
description_long: G Bangkok competes with DJ Station for the circuit and EDM crowd on the Silom strip. Multiple floors and strong sound system. Known for themed nights and a younger, more energetic crowd than its neighbours.

name: Telephone Pub
description_short: Classic Silom Soi 4 institution with table-top phone gimmick and easy-going local crowd.
description_long: Telephone Pub is one of the original Silom Soi 4 bars, known for the table-top phones you can use to call other tables. A relaxed, unpretentious atmosphere popular with tourists and long-term Bangkok visitors.

name: The Stranger Bar
description_short: Laid-back Silom Soi 4 bar with strong pours and a mixed local and tourist crowd nightly.
description_long: The Stranger Bar sits in the thick of Silom Soi 4 and delivers exactly what it promises — a no-frills, casual gay bar with cheap drinks and a friendly mix of tourists and regulars.

name: Bearbie
description_short: Dedicated bear bar on Silom Soi 2 with a welcoming masculine crowd.
description_long: Bearbie is Bangkok's go-to spot for the bear community. A relaxed, no-pretence bar atmosphere with a loyal local following. One of the few venues on the Silom strip catering specifically to a masculine/bear crowd.

name: Babylon Bangkok
description_short: Bangkok's most established gay sauna, known for its well-maintained facilities in Sathorn.
description_long: Babylon has been Bangkok's premier gay sauna for decades. Set in a quieter Sathorn side street, it offers multiple floors of sauna, steam, pool, and massage facilities. Popular with both locals and international visitors.

name: Fake Club
description_short: Popular Ratchadapisek club with a queer-friendly following and high-energy weekend nights.
description_long: Fake Club operates in the Ratchadapisek entertainment corridor, drawing a mixed crowd for its weekend club nights. Known for drag performances and chart-heavy music policy.

name: Disco Bangkok
description_short: No-frills Silom Soi 4 bar with retro décor and an easy-going mixed crowd.
description_long: Disco Bangkok is one of the original bars on Silom Soi 4, with a retro interior and laid-back atmosphere. Good for a first drink before heading deeper into the strip.

name: Adam Bangkok
description_short: Small, welcoming bar on Silom Soi 2 catering to a bear and casual queer crowd.
description_long: Adam Bangkok is a smaller, character-driven bar on Silom Soi 2. Known for friendly staff and a regular local crowd. Good complement to the bigger clubs on the same strip.

name: ARQ Sydney
description_short: Sydney's largest gay nightclub, a Darlinghurst institution running since the 1990s.
description_long: ARQ is the beating heart of Sydney's gay nightlife. Over three levels including a main dance floor, mezzanine bar, and outdoor terrace, it draws a mixed crowd for its pop and circuit nights. Synonymous with Sydney's Mardi Gras season.

name: Stonewall Hotel
description_short: Oxford Street cornerstone with multiple bars, drag shows, and a friendly all-day vibe.
description_long: The Stonewall Hotel has held down a corner of Oxford Street for decades, offering drag shows, DJs, and an all-day pub atmosphere. One of the few venues that genuinely serves the full spectrum of the Oxford Street crowd from afternoon to late night.

name: The Beresford Hotel
description_short: Beloved Surry Hills pub with a large rooftop terrace and a strongly queer-friendly crowd.
description_long: The Beresford is a Sydney institution that blends a traditional pub feel with genuine LGBTQ+ warmth. The rooftop space is particularly popular on weekends. Slightly removed from the main Oxford Street strip, it has a loyal local following.

name: Palms on Oxford
description_short: Oxford Street drag bar known for regular performances and a laid-back welcome-all vibe.
description_long: Palms on Oxford serves as a reliable drag venue on the main Oxford Street strip. Regular performer nights draw a mixed crowd of tourists and locals. One of the more affordable options on the strip.

name: The Colombian Hotel
description_short: Multi-level Oxford Street pub with a long history and a casual door policy.
description_long: The Colombian Hotel is one of the larger pubs on Oxford Street, with multiple levels of bars and a large outdoor terrace. More casual than some neighbours, it draws a broad mixed crowd from afternoon through to late night.

name: Oxford Hotel
description_short: Stylish Oxford Street pub with rooftop bar and a smart queer crowd.
description_long: The Oxford Hotel is a step up from the strip's more casual pubs. A cocktail-forward rooftop bar and well-maintained interior attract a slightly older, more design-conscious crowd. Popular for early evening gatherings.

name: Shift
description_short: Late-night club at the Oxford Street end with a pop and house music policy.
description_long: Shift is one of the dedicated nightclub options near Oxford Street, drawing a younger crowd for its weekend late-night sessions. Known for an affordable entry price and high-energy dance floor.

name: Club Kooky
description_short: Kings Cross drag club known for its wild themed parties and performance-heavy programming.
description_long: Club Kooky operates in the Kings Cross area, slightly off the main Oxford Street strip. Known for elaborate drag performances and themed nights, it draws a younger creative crowd.

name: Gingers Bar
description_short: Newtown queer bar off the main Oxford Street strip with an alternative, artsy crowd.
description_long: Gingers Bar sits in Newtown's creative heartland, serving a queer crowd that gravitates toward the alternative end of the spectrum. A welcome counterpoint to the Oxford Street mainstream, drawing performers and regulars.

name: Toolshed
description_short: Sydney's main gay sauna, well-established in Bondi Junction with regular clientele.
description_long: Toolshed is Sydney's most prominent gay sauna, located in Bondi Junction. Facilities include sauna, steam room, and social areas. Popular with both locals and visitors seeking a dedicated social space.

name: The Week São Paulo
description_short: Brazil's biggest gay club, hosting international circuit parties every weekend in São Paulo.
description_long: The Week is South America's most prominent gay club, attracting thousands of visitors for its circuit nights. Set in a massive warehouse-style space in Bom Retiro, it hosts international DJs and is the anchor of São Paulo's gay nightlife calendar.

name: Blue Space
address: Rua Brigadeiro Galvão 723, São Paulo SP 01151-000
description_short: São Paulo's main drag club, known for spectacular shows and a loyal mixed crowd.
description_long: Blue Space is famous for its drag performances, which are among the most elaborate in Brazil. Multiple floors cater to different music tastes, from pop to electronic. A mainstay of São Paulo's gay nightlife.

name: Club A
description_short: Consolação nightclub with a queer-friendly reputation and strong weekend programming.
description_long: Club A is one of several clubs in São Paulo's Consolação entertainment district that draws a queer-friendly crowd. Known for its weekend nights featuring a mix of commercial house and pop.

name: Le Boy
description_short: São Paulo gay club in the Jardins area with drag shows and a loyal weekend crowd.
description_long: Le Boy has been part of São Paulo's gay scene for years, offering a more intimate alternative to The Week with drag shows and pop-heavy programming. Located in the upmarket Jardins neighbourhood.

name: Bubu Lounge
address: Rua Atílio Iório 53, São Paulo SP 01311-100
description_short: Jardins cocktail lounge with a queer-friendly atmosphere and regular themed nights.
description_long: Bubu Lounge is a more intimate option in São Paulo's upmarket Jardins area. Cocktail-focused with a warm queer-friendly atmosphere, it caters to a slightly older professional crowd.

name: Clube Yacht São Paulo
address: Rua Leopoldo Couto de Magalhães Jr 1048, São Paulo SP 04542-001
description_short: Landmark São Paulo circuit club event brand, running major Saturday nights since the 1990s.
description_long: Clube Yacht is one of São Paulo's most storied circuit party brands, regularly drawing thousands to its Saturday nights. International DJs and a well-heeled crowd define the experience.

name: Pacha São Paulo
address: Avenida do Cursino 1400, São Paulo SP
description_short: São Paulo outpost of the international Pacha brand, hosting large circuit and pop events.
description_long: Pacha São Paulo operates as a large-format event venue for circuit parties and mainstream club nights. Its location in the Zona Sul is slightly removed from the Jardins/Lapa core but draws significant crowds for major events.

name: Bar Balcão
description_short: Casual neighbourhood bar in Jardins with a queer-mixed crowd and regular themed nights.
description_long: Bar Balcão is a low-key neighbourhood spot in São Paulo's Jardins area, popular with a queer crowd for its unpretentious atmosphere and strong cocktail list.

name: Phoenix Club SP
description_short: Pinheiros nightclub with a circuit following and weekend dance nights.
description_long: Phoenix Club SP operates in the Pinheiros area and draws a circuit-leaning crowd for its weekend nights. Limited online presence makes verification difficult.

name: Dom São Paulo
address: Rua Leopoldo Couto de Magalhães Jr 1048, São Paulo SP 04542-001
description_short: Upscale Itaim Bibi bar with a stylish queer-mixed crowd and strong cocktail programme.
description_long: Dom São Paulo caters to an affluent queer crowd in the upmarket Itaim Bibi neighbourhood. Design-forward space with a premium cocktail menu and a social scene that flows into the circuit party world.

name: Boy Bar
description_short: Zona Rosa drag bar and dance venue, one of Mexico City's most popular gay destinations.
description_long: Boy Bar is the heartbeat of Mexico City's Zona Rosa gay scene, offering nightly drag shows and pop music across multiple floors. Consistently busy and welcoming to both tourists and locals.

name: El Taller
description_short: Mexico City's main leather and bear bar, tucked into Zona Rosa with a loyal local crowd.
description_long: El Taller is Zona Rosa's dedicated leather and bear bar, a no-frills venue with a dark interior and a masculine crowd. A counterpoint to the pop-oriented bars nearby, it draws a local following.

name: Kinky Bar
description_short: High-energy Zona Rosa nightclub with drag shows and a pop-heavy dance floor.
description_long: Kinky Bar is one of the larger venues in Mexico City's Zona Rosa, consistently drawing a mixed crowd for its drag performances and pop club nights. Well-known to both visitors and regulars.

name: Patrick Miller
description_short: Iconic Mexico City disco in Doctores, famous for 80s pop and an enthusiastic queer crowd.
description_long: Patrick Miller is a Mexico City institution that predates the modern gay bar scene. The unassuming venue in Doctores neighbourhood has built a cult following for its retro pop and 80s-heavy programming. Genuinely queer-friendly rather than specifically gay.

name: La Mission
description_short: Zona Rosa bar with regular drag entertainment and a casual mixed crowd.
description_long: La Mission operates in the same Zona Rosa block as several other gay venues, benefiting from foot traffic. Known for drag shows at weekends.

name: Lipstick
description_short: Zona Rosa club on the corner of Amberes, known for pop nights and drag performances.
description_long: Lipstick is centrally located in Zona Rosa and draws a mainstream crowd for its pop and drag programming. A good option for first-time visitors wanting a reliable, active club night.

name: Marrakech Salón
description_short: Historic Centro Histórico drag salon, one of Mexico City's most distinctive queer venues.
description_long: Marrakech Salón is a Mexico City original, a drag and cabaret salon operating in the Centro Histórico. Known for its theatrical drag performances and a diverse local crowd, it occupies a unique cultural position outside the Zona Rosa mainstream.

name: Tom's Leather Bar
description_short: Zona Rosa leather bar serving a masculine crowd with minimal frills and late hours.
description_long: Tom's Leather Bar is the Zona Rosa option for those seeking a masculine, leather-leaning atmosphere. Small, dark interior and a regular crowd that values privacy.

name: Albur Bar
description_short: Low-key Zona Rosa bar with a queer-mixed crowd and occasional drag entertainment.
description_long: Albur Bar is one of several smaller bars along Calle Génova in Zona Rosa. A casual neighbourhood spot with limited online presence, recommended for independent research before visiting.

name: La Cantina Gay
description_short: All-day cantina bar in Zona Rosa serving a mixed queer crowd from early afternoon.
description_long: La Cantina Gay is an approachable option in Zona Rosa for early-evening drinks. Open long hours and welcoming to all, though limited online presence means details require manual verification.

name: Twin Peaks Tavern
description_short: The Castro's most iconic gay bar, famous for floor-to-ceiling windows since 1972.
description_long: Twin Peaks Tavern holds a unique place in LGBTQ+ history as the first gay bar in the US to have large plate-glass windows, a deliberate act of visibility in 1972. Today it remains a Castro cornerstone, attracting a mixed local crowd from afternoon onwards.

name: The Lookout
description_short: Elevated Castro bar with a large open terrace and popular happy hour.
description_long: The Lookout sits above the Castro's main intersection at 16th and Market, with a large open terrace that attracts a mixed queer crowd from early afternoon. One of the area's busiest bars for happy hour.

name: Moby Dick
description_short: Classic Castro dive bar open daily, known for its fish tank and strong local regulars.
description_long: Moby Dick is a Castro institution that defies trends. A dive bar in the best sense, with a large fish tank and a loyal regular crowd. Open from noon daily, it draws the Castro's working-class queer contingent.

name: SF Eagle
description_short: San Francisco's oldest leather bar, anchoring SoMa's queer leather scene since 1981.
description_long: The SF Eagle has been a SoMa landmark since 1981 and remains the city's pre-eminent leather and bear bar. The outdoor patio is a social hub, and the Sunday Beer Bust is a long-running tradition drawing hundreds of regulars.

name: Powerhouse
description_short: Folsom Street leather bar with a dark, no-pretence interior and strong SoMa pedigree.
description_long: Powerhouse has held its Folsom Street spot for decades, serving the SoMa leather community with a reliably masculine atmosphere. Dark interior, cheap drinks, and a loyal crowd of regulars define the experience.

name: Midnight Sun
description_short: Castro video bar showing music videos continuously, a neighbourhood staple since 1973.
description_long: Midnight Sun is one of the Castro's long-running video bars, playing music videos on large screens in a relaxed pub atmosphere. A neighbourhood staple with a mixed queer crowd and reliable happy hour specials.

name: Oasis

opening_hours.notes: Event-based Wed–Sat; check sfoasis.com for schedule

description_short: SF's premier drag and cabaret venue, home to Mother and other flagship queer productions.
description_long: Oasis is SF's most important queer performance venue, hosting a rotating calendar of drag shows, cabarets, and club nights. Best known as the home of the weekly Mother party. A polished production-quality environment.

name: Beaux
description_short: Smart Castro cocktail bar at the top of Market Street with a friendly neighbourhood feel.
description_long: Beaux sits at the Market Street end of the Castro, offering a slightly more cocktail-forward experience than the strip's traditional dives. Popular for after-work drinks and a loyal regular crowd.

name: Badlands
description_short: Castro video bar open daily, known for chart music and a relaxed mixed crowd.
description_long: Badlands is a Castro video bar that has been showing pop music videos and drawing a mixed crowd since the 1990s. Unpretentious, open long hours, and reliably busy on weekends.

name: QBar
description_short: Castro bar with a cocktail-forward menu and a welcoming mixed neighbourhood crowd.
description_long: QBar occupies a Castro Street slot with a reputation for good cocktails and a relaxed atmosphere. Limited online presence; details require manual verification.
